compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visually identifiable as living specimens within the provided frame, suggesting either systemic loss or complete dormancy/burial.

plant_audit.p3: Single leaf with petiole, stable posture.
plant_audit.soil_surface: Contains crushed eggshell fragments, likely a calcium-based intervention.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently moist/dark, no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells across the soil surface of P3.

temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-4: Loss of visual confirmation of P2/P4.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_current: Static state; no growth or further decline observed in the remaining specimens.

visual_health_inference.p3: Stable, no signs of chlorosis or necrosis.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: Stagnant. The lack of new growth over 5 days suggests a period of dormancy or environmental stress.

anomalies: Crushed eggshell fragments in P3 soil (New Intervention).
Absence of P2 and P4 (Systemic Loss).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of stasis. The Pothos (P3) and String of Nickels (P1) remain the only visible occupants. The introduction of crushed eggshells indicates an attempt at soil amendment, though no biological response is yet visible. The absence of the Mexican Mint and Silver Guest suggests these specimens have either perished or were removed from the frame entirely. The environment appears stable but lacks active vegetative progression.
